reasoning_content_list:
We need to extract info from the markdown about ecosystem-related study features. Fields: habitat, natural_region, climate, landuse. The document is a policy info, not a study, but we must extract any mentions of these categories.

We need "ausschließlich gültiges JSON". Provide only fields: habitat: array maybe empty. natural_region: array values: which natural regions mentioned? "Norddeutschen Tiefebene" (Norddeutsches Tiefland). "Alpenvorland" also. Possibly "Alpen". Also "Mittelgebirgsschwelle" maybe not explicitly. The text mentions "Alpenvorland" in 1st paragraph. Also "Norddeutsches Tiefland" as "norddeutschen Tiefebene". Need to check allowed values.

Habitat: allowed values: Agrar- und Offenland, Binnengewässer und Auen, Boden, Küsten und Küstengewässer, Urbane Räume, Wald. The text mentions Moorböden which are "Boden" type. Could also mention agrar land, because used agriculturally. So habitat could include Boden, Agrar- und Offenland. Also "Wasser" but that's Binnengewässer und Auen maybe not.

Climate: allowed values: Atlantisches Klima, Gebirgsklima, kontinental, maritim, niederschlagsbenachteiligt, niederschlagsnormal, niederschlagsbegünstigt, subkontinental, submaritim. The text says "Klimaschutz", but no explicit climate type described. There might be implied "küstenn"... The region is North Germany, "Norddeutsches Tiefland" would have a "kontinental" or "maritimes"? Actually North German plain has a "kontinental" atmosphere? Let’s guess "kontinental" is possible. But no mention.

Landuse: many options. They mention "Landwirtschaft", "Brachliegende Flächen", "Forstwirtschaft"? Not here. Land used: "landwirtschaftlich genutzt". Also "Rein" - "forster"? Not. Also "Bau" maybe? There is PVA installations (Solar) which is "Bau". Also "Industrie und Fertigung" not; "Energieproduktion" maybe "Energieproduktion" because it's PV. Also "Wohngebiete"? no. "Öffentliche Einrichtungen" maybe not.

Important: we must provide JSON with only specified fields as arrays (or empty arrays). Cardinality 0..* means they can be empty. So we need to include all four fields. For any categories not present but presumably present in text we include. The document explicitly mentions land use: "Entwässerung", "landwirtschaftlich genutzt", "PVA" -> energy production. Also "Bau" maybe. Also "Anlagen". We can include "Landwirtschaft" and "Energieproduktion" and "Bau". Check allowed values: Bau, Bergbau und Steinbruch, Brachliegende Flächen, Energieproduktion, Erholung, Freizeit, Sport, Fischerei und Aquakultur, Forstwirtschaft, Handels-, Finanz-, Berufs- und Informationsdienstleistungen, Industrie und Fertigung, Landwirtschaft, Naturnahe und natürliche Flächen, die nicht genutzt werden, Verkehr, Kommunikationsnetzwerke, Lagerung, Schutzwälle, Wasser- und Abfallbehandlung, Wohngebiete, andere Leistung des Primärsektors, Öffentliche Einrichtungen.

Thus relevant ones: Bau, Energieproduktion, Landwirtschaft. Also maybe "Brachliegende Flächen" if any. I think just those three.

Habitat: Moorböden => Boden. Also "Agrar- und Offenland" because they are used agriculturally. So include both. Maybe "Binnengewässer und Auen" if mention of "Moore" but not. Should we include "Küsten und Küstengewässer"? Not mentioned. "Urbane Räume"? None. "Wald": not mentioned. So habitat array: ["Boden", "Agrar- und Offenland"].

Natural_region: In the text: "nörddeutschen Tiefebene" -> "Norddeutsches Tiefland". "Alpenvorland". Also "Alpen" maybe not explicitly but "Alpenvorland" explicitly. So include both: "Norddeutsches Tiefland", "Alpenvorland". Are there others? "Mittelgebirgsschwelle" not mentioned. So those two.

Climate: Not explicit. So probably empty array. But we should maybe include none. Provide empty array.

Landuse: include ["Bau", "Energieproduktion", "Landwirtschaft"]. It's about policy about PVA, so "Bau" because building, "Energieproduktion" because PV, "Landwirtschaft" because land used. Also maybe "Brachliegende Flächen"? It says "Moorböden ... landwirtschaftlich genutzt". "Brachliegende Flächen" not. So three.

Thus final JSON:

{
"habitat": ["Boden","Agrar- und Offenland"],
"natural_region": ["Norddeutsches Tiefland","Alpenvorland"],
"climate": [],
"landuse": ["Bau","Energieproduktion","Landwirtschaft"]
}

Check field names: lowerCamelCase? The schema says: habitat, natural_region, climate, landuse. All lower case. Provide as array of strings. Provide valid JSON; no comments.
